---
title: "Movie Survival Calculator Using MBTI Types"
subtitle: "GLHLTH 562 Final Project"
author: "Zada Tiimob & Khalid Tejan-Sie"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ["night", "custom.scss"]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    background-transition: fade
    code-line-numbers: false
    center: false
---

## Product Idea

- Guess how long your MBTI type survives in three movies.
- Pull movie plots and genres from <span class="orange">OMDb</span>.
- Use <span class="orange">MBTI traits</span> + genres + plot words to estimate:
  - Chance of dying in the first half
  - Chance of dying by the end
- Optional <span class="orange">ChatGPT</span> scoring.

## Inputs

- MBTI type (16 options)
- Three movie titles
- Optional: “Use ChatGPT-based scoring” toggle

## Data Sources

- **OMDb API**
  - Search by title: `?t=MovieTitle&plot=full`
  - Uses: `Plot`, `Genre`, `Runtime`, `Year`, `imdbRating`
- **MBTI descriptors**
  - Four dichotomies (E/I, S/N, T/F, J/P)
  - Type descriptions (ex: INTJ = strategic/independent)

## Text & Trait Pipeline

- Split plot into words with <span class="orange">tidytext</span>
- Match words to <span class="orange">trait keywords</span>
- Add <span class="orange">genre risk weights</span> (Horror > Comedy)

## Scoring Overview

1. Start with a baseline
2. Add genre effects
3. Add MBTI trait effects
4. Add plot keyword matches
5. Lower scores for light genres


## AI Scoring

- Calls <span class="orange">OpenAI</span>
- Inputs: <span class="orange">MBTI traits</span>, plot, genre, rule-based scores
- Outputs JSON with probabilities

## Tools Used

- **R / Shiny**
- `httr`, `jsonlite` for API calls
- `tidytext`, `stringr`, `dplyr` for text processing

## Next Steps

- Optimize extraction of keywords from plot summaries
- Allow input of 1-3 movies
- Added sensitivity to grammatical or spelling errors for movies (drop-down menu of candidate movies)
